title: Hemionitis paupercula
intro: Hemionitis paupercula, also known as the small maidenhair fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Blechnaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, as well as its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Hemionitis paupercula include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are often used in traditional medicine to alleviate various health conditions.

The classification of Hemionitis paupercula is part of a larger system of classification that includes kingdom, phylum, class, order, family, genus, and species.
